channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: They were there, but barely: Celebs embrace naked fashion trend at Oscar after-parties
item.description: BEVERLY HILLS, Calif. (AP) — At the Oscars ceremony, they were there. At the after-parties, they were barely there. Celebrities bared a lot more than their emotions on Oscar night.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: AG Pam Bondi says FBI delivered 'truckload' of Epstein files after she put out hard deadline
item.description: AG Pam Bondi said the FBI handed over a "truckload" of Epstein files after she gave the agency until 8 a.m. Friday morning to turn them over. She also praised FBI boss Kash Patel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pauses US military aid to Ukraine while pressuring Zelenskyy to move toward quick end to war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday directed a “pause” to U.S. assistance to Ukraine as he seeks to pressure Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to engage in negotiations to end the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kim's sister threatens a response to a US carrier's deployment in South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un threatened Tuesday to launch high-profile provocations in response to the arrival of a U.S. aircraft carrier in South Korea and other U.S. military activities, which she slammed as “confrontation hysteria of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: GOP bill to ban trans athletes from women’s sports blocked in Senate
item.description: Legislation to prevent transgender athletes from participating in girls’ and women’s sports failed to advance in the Senate on Monday after all Democrats voted against it. The Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act failed to clear an initial procedural hurdle on a 51-45 vote.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: Second email to federal workers comes with caveat
item.description: On Friday, the Office of Personnel Management sent an email asking employees to list five bullets' worth of accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 leaders of a group suspected of smuggling 20,000 immigrants are arrested in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrested two alleged leaders of a criminal organization suspected of smuggling 20,000 people from Guatemala to the U.S. and charging each person as much as $18,000 to get them into the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP rep introduces measure putting Trump face on $100 bill
item.description: First-term GOP Rep. Brandon Gill (Texas) has introduced a measure that would require all $100 bills to have President Trump’s face on them.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: How did Americans view the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting? Poll shows stark divide
item.description: About half of those polled said neither Ukraine nor the U.S. walked away as a winner from last week's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Black graves are being moved to make way for an industrial park, drawing a mix of emotions
item.description: DANVILLE, Va. (AP) — A decision to move the remains of hundreds African American tenant farmers from a former Virginia tobacco plantation to a dedicated burial ground has elicited a range of emotions among the sharecroppers’ descendants.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illinois couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KRON4

item.title: Ariana Grande goes full Glinda, 'Wicked' bestie Erivo does Elphaba proud in green on Oscars carpet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ariana Grande did her Glinda proud in a pale pink stunner, and her “Wicked” bestie Cynthia Erivo honored Elphaba in deep forest green on the Oscars carpet as high glam took over Sunday. Others went for bold red, classic black and pops of pink.
item.dc:creator: LEANNE ITALIE and BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California Highway Patrol blames drug use, speeding for fiery Cybertruck crash that killed 3
item.description: PIEDMONT, Calif. (AP) — Drug use and excessive speed were to blame for last year's fiery crash of a Tesla Cybertruck that killed three college students near San Francisco, the California Highway Patrol said in a preliminary report.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bondi says she was misled on Epstein documents
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi says she was misled into believing she had all the files related to the Epstein investigations, and is now seeking additional information from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Youngkin grants clemency to a fired Virginia police sergeant who shot and killed an unarmed man
item.description: Virginia Gov. Glenn Youngkin has granted clemency to a fired police sergeant who shot and killed an unarmed man accused of stealing sunglasses.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at 97th Academy Awards
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kroger CEO resigns amid personal conduct investigation
item.description: McMullen's departure comes months after Kroger's failed two-year effort to merge with another supermarket giant, Albertsons.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, KRON4

item.title: Singer Angie Stone killed in car crash after leaving Alabama performance
item.description: Stone was riding in the back of a vehicle that "flipped over and subsequently hit by a big rig," according to family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, Fox 5

item.title: Rubio signals Zelenskyy now willing to sign Trump's minerals deal and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 175 wildfires in North and South Carolina force some evacuations
item.description: Wildfires in North and South Carolina have scorched thousands of acres as crews battling more than 175 blazes face dry and windy conditions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Photos: The 2025 Oscars Red Carpet in Los Angeles
item.description: Hollywood's biggest stars and most talented artists are set to be honored Sunday evening during the 97th Academy Awards held at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood. But before the awards ceremony itself begins, the stars are walking the iconic Red Carpet where fashion takes center stage.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michigan mom seeks 'respect' for her 3 missing boys, asks a judge to declare them dead
item.description: A Michigan woman has directly asked a judge to declare her three missing sons dead nearly 15 years after they disappeared.
item.dc:creator: Ed White, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Watch the full Trump-Zelenskyy Oval Office meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump shouted at Ukraine's leader on Friday during an extraordinary meeting in the Oval Office, berating President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for “gambling with millions of lives” and suggesting his actions could trigger World War III.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: To her, Hurricane Helene debris isn’t trash. It is full of memories
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — The tops of dried, bent cornstalks crunch underfoot. Jill Holtz’s gaze is fixed on the ground ahead. She wanders into the nearby woods and weaves between twisted branches. Then, Holtz spots something and starts to riffle through the withered twigs.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US lobster catch drops as crustaceans migrate to colder Canadian waters
item.description: SCARBOROUGH, Maine (AP) — The U.S. lobster industry's catch keeps sliding as fishermen contend with the northward migration of the valuable crustaceans. The industry is based mostly in Maine, where lobsters are both a cultural signifier and the backbone of the coastal economy.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Christianity in US stops decline after years of losses: Survey
item.description: Although down substantially from 2007, 44 percent of Americans said they pray at least once a day, a figure that has remained consistent in the last four years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9
